data.title: Seatrium awarded offshore scope for TenneT’s BalWin5 project
data.description:
The fabrication starts January 2026 for the 2.2-GW HVDC project.

Seatrium has been awarded the offshore scope for TenneT’s BalWin5 grid connection project as part of a consortium with GE Vernova, according to the press release. 

The project covers a 2.2-gigawatt high-voltage direct current (HVDC) link between the German North Sea and Germany’s onshore grid and is scheduled for commissioning in 2032.

The grid connection will span about 325 kilometres across subsea and onshore cables and is designed with 200 magawatts of technical headroom above the standard 2GW capacity. The system is stated to be capable of supplying electricity to approximately 2.75 million households.

Seatrium will design, construct, transport and install the offshore converter platform, with fabrication to be carried out in Singapore and Batam starting on 1 January 2026. GE Vernova will provide the HVDC technology, including onshore and offshore converter stations and control and protection systems.

The BalWin5 award marks the fourth project secured by the GE Vernova–Seatrium consortium under a five-year framework cooperation agreement with TenneT announced in March 2023, the press release said. It is the first project awarded under TenneT’s German 2GW offshore grid connection programme.

data.title: IMDA fines Singtel $1m over fixed voice disruption in October 2024
data.description:
Singtel said shared memory for its voice and broadband was overwhelmed by high traffic.

The Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) imposed a penalty of $1m on Singapore Telecommunications Limited (Singtel) for a disruption incident that affected about 500,000 of Singtel users in October 2024.

The incident affected about Singtel’s residential and corporate users for more than four hours, IMDA said.

The public’s access to customer service lines for some government agencies, healthcare organisations, banks and companies, and emergency call services was also affected,  it added.

IMDA found that Singtel operated two virtualised firewalls on the same hardware, one for its fixed-line voice system and another for home broadband routers and pay TV set-top boxes.

Because both firewalls shared the same hardware memory resources, a failure in one system had implications for the other, the investigation found.

When traffic surged on 8 October 2024, the hardware’s shared memory resources were overwhelmed and caused the voice system’s firewall to malfunction and operate intermittently, it added.

The affected voice system was designed with an automatic failover mechanism that should have redirected voice traffic to a separate system, IMDA said, adding that this did not occur as intended because the virtualised firewall on the affected system was operating intermittently.

The issue was only resolved after Singtel fully transferred all voice traffic to the unaffected voice system.

data.title: Sembcorp to acquire Alinta Energy in $5b transaction
data.description:
Completion is expected in the first half of 2026.

Sembcorp Industries will acquire 100% of Alinta Energy at an enterprise value of over $5b (A$5.9b), subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions, according to a joint announcement by the companies. Completion of the transaction is expected in the first half of 2026.

Alinta Energy operates electricity and gas retail businesses in Australia and reported fleet availability of 93%. The company also has a development pipeline of 10.4GW comprising renewable and firming capacity.

Following completion, Alinta Energy will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Sembcorp Industries. The acquisition will be funded through a combination of internal resources and external financing.

The acquisition remains subject to approvals from relevant regulatory authorities.

1 AUD = 0.86 SGD

data.title: Grab Vietnam partners Charge+ as nationwide EV charging rollout begins
data.description:
Under the collaboration, Grab will integrate Charge+’s network into its app via API.

Grab Vietnam has signed an agreement with regional EV-charging provider Charge+ to roll out a nationwide network of electric vehicle charging and battery-swapping stations.

The infrastructure will support cars, motorcycles, and e-scooters, with priority access for Grab driver-partners but availability for other EV users as well.

Both companies said the partnership is aimed at providing flexible, reliable, and fast charging to reduce downtime for drivers and to support Vietnam’s broader net-zero transition.

Under the collaboration, Grab will integrate Charge+’s network into its app via API, allowing driver-partners to view real-time charger locations and availability, and to handle payments seamlessly.

Network deployment will be guided by Grab’s demand data and operational footprint, with co-siting at locations expected to generate the highest utilisation.

Charge+ has set a target of installing 5,000 charging points in Vietnam by 2030. Grab driver-partners will receive preferential charging rates.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 0.2%; Top stock is HongkongLand USD
data.description:
HongkongLand USD is the top stock with a 5.48% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,520.83, up 0.2% on Thursday, 11 December, at 5:21 p.m.

HongkongLand USD was the most active stock, followed by YZJ Shipbldg SGD (+2.66%) and Seatrium Ltd(+0.97%).

Mapletree Log Tr and UOL were at the foot.

data.title: ComfortDelGro receives licence to conduct public road trials for AV shuttles
data.description:
This covers testing of five autonomous shuttles in Punggol in coming months.

ComfortDelGro received approval from the Land Transport Authority to begin public road trials of its autonomous-vehicle shuttles after the first batch of vehicles completed Milestone 1 (M1) assessments by the Centre of Testing & Research of Autonomous Vehicles (CETRAN), according to a press release.

Group CEO Cheng Siak Kian said the successful M1 assessment fulfilled the standards required for the next phase of autonomous-vehicle testing.

ComfortDelGro said it plans to make AV rides available in 2026 through its CDG Zig app under a “Zig Driverless” option.

The company will start trials of five seven-seater AV shuttles in the Punggol district.

data.title: High Court OKs mm2 Asia's four-month moratorium request
data.description:
No legal actions may be taken against the company during this period. 

No legal or administrative proceedings may be taken against Mm2 Asia Ltd. for four months after 10 December, its board of directors said after the High Court approved its application to seek a moratorium.

The court ordered that no proceeding whether before a court, arbitral tribunal or administrative agency "shall be commenced or continued against the company, except with the leave of the Court and subject to such terms as the Court may impose," according to a bourse filing. 

The moratorium also includes execution, distress or other legal process, as well as any step to enforce security over any property of the company and the right to re-entry of forfeiture under any lease. 

The directive also limits the company from passing resolutions and making appointments during the four-month period.

data.title: Total employment up by 25,100 in Q3
data.description:
Resident jobless rate reaches 3.1% as retrenchments ease

Overall employment increased by 25,100 in Q3, stronger than the 10,400 increase seen in the previous quarter, according to the Labour Market Report of the Ministry of Manpower.  

Employment among residents was led by the financial and insurace services, and health and social services sectors, while employment among non-residents was driven mainly by the construction and manufacturing sector. 

The ministry added unemployment rates stayed low during the quarter, while the number of job vacancies eased slightly to 69,200 in September from 76,900 in June. 

The ministry registered 1.6 retrenchments per 1,000 employees, with the total number of retrenchments at 3,670 during the quarter.

data.title: CapitaLand Investment closes second onshore sub-fund under RMB Master Fund
data.description:
It is expected to add $271m (RMB1.48b) to CLI's funds. 

CapitaLand Investment Limited (CLI) closed its second onshore subfund China Retail RMB Fund I (CRF I), under its RMB Master Fund, to bring CLI’s total fund size to $183m (RMB1b).

The CRF I is part of the CLI RMB Master Fund and is expected to add $271m (RMB1.48b) to CLI’s funds under management (FUM) when fully deployed, CLI said in a bourse filing.

CLI will recapitalise CapitaMall Xinduxin through its sale as the seed asset to CRF I, it added.

Located in Qingdao’s Shibei District, CapitaMall Xinduxin is a prime retail asset with a gross floor area of 141,000 square metres and a committed occupancy of approximately 99.6%.

The shopping mall is connected to Qingdao’s subway line 3.

Following the second fund, CLI has raised nearly $10b (RMB55b) of domestic capital across nine onshore funds since 2021, it said.

data.title: Ever Glory United launches public offer of up to 2 million shares
data.description:
The offer opens 11 December ahead of expected listing.

Ever Glory United opened a public offer of up to 2 million shares at $0.64 each on 11 December 2025, according to the company’s offer advertisement. 

Applications will close at noon on 17 December 2025, whilst balloting is scheduled for 18 December 2025.

The group provides mechanical and electrical engineering services for public and private sector developments, including residential, commercial and industrial projects. Its operations include acquired entities Fire-Guard Engineering Pte. Ltd. and Guthrie Engineering Pte. Ltd., which broaden its service capabilities.

The company said its business activities include engineering services alongside a small property development and investment segment. It noted that potential expansion through joint ventures, mergers, acquisitions or alliances would depend on market conditions and available opportunities.

Applications may be submitted through ATMs, internet banking or mobile banking interfaces of OCBC and UOB, or via printed forms. Trading of the shares is expected to begin on 19 December 2025 on a ready basis, with settlement slated for 23 December 2025.
